While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=draft}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|12=draft}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|12=draft}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|会津美里町|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=draft}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taue Odori|ja|田植踊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|12=draft}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=draft}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=draft}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|12=draft}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=draft}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=draft}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|12=draft}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|12=draft}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=draft}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|12=draft}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=draft}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=draft}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|12=draft}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|12=draft}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|会津美里町|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=draft}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taue Odori|ja|田植踊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|12=draft}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

revision.text:
[[ファイル:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|[[香取神宮御田植祭]]]] '''御田植祭'''（おたうえまつり）は、[[寺社]]や[[皇室]]の[[御田|領田]]で行われる行事である。
== 概要 == [[農作業]]の工程を模擬的に演じ[[豊作]]を祈念する[[神事]]であり、実際に[[田植え]]の時期に行われるものを御田植祭と呼び、[[正月|新春]]の[[予祝芸能]]を[[田遊び]]と呼ぶのが一般的であるが、田遊びを含め御田植祭と呼ばれることも多い。
この種の行事は全国に[[伝承]]されており、[[日本人]]と[[稲作]]との長く深い係わりを示すものとされる。地域によって行事を行う組織は異なるが、収穫までの全生産過程を演じる例は少なく、田植で終わる「御田植祭」が多いといわれる。
== 起源 == 苦しい田植えの作業を楽しくする方法として[[田植歌]]を歌いながら田植えをするという[[風習]]が、[[田の神]]を祀って豊穣を願う農耕儀礼と結びついて[[祭礼]]となったのが起源とされるが、歴史的には[[権門]]による勧農があり、その模倣や展開の結果として多くの地域で行われるようになったともいわれる。このため、地域を代表する古くからの寺社の[[年中行事]]として伝えられる場合が多く、いずれも農作業の工程を模擬的に演ずることが中心だが、地域ごとに種々の芸能の要素が伝えられ、特徴ある行事が伝承されている。
== 代表的な御田植祭 == === 日本三大御田植 === * [[千葉県]][[香取市]]香取の[[香取神宮]]の[[香取神宮御田植祭]]<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html 香取神宮の御田植祭] 香取市</ref> * [[三重県]][[志摩市]]の[[伊雑宮]]の御田植（[[磯部の御神田]]）（[[重要無形民俗文化財]]）<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 伊雑宮 御田植祭／日本三大御田植祭（重要無形民俗文化財）] 志摩市観光協会</ref> * [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]住吉の[[住吉大社]]の[[住吉の御田植]]（重要無形民俗文化財）<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== その他の御田植 === * [[福島県]][[東白川郡]][[棚倉町]]八槻の[[都々古別神社の御田植]]（重要無形民俗文化財） * 福島県[[喜多方市]]の[[慶徳稲荷神社]]の御田植祭（重要無形民俗文化財） * 福島県[[大沼郡]][[会津美里町]]の[[伊佐須美神社]]の御田植祭（重要無形民俗文化財） * [[埼玉県]][[秩父市]]番場町の[[秩父神社]]の[[秩父神社御田植祭]]（[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|埼玉県指定無形民俗文化財]]） * [[富山県]][[射水市]]の[[加茂神社 (射水市)|下村加茂神社]]の御田植祭（[[富山県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|富山県指定無形民俗文化財]]、[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）に「とやまの文化財百選（とやまの祭り百選部門）」に選定） * [[兵庫県]][[姫路市]][[広嶺山]]の[[広峯神社]]の御田植祭（姫路市指定無形民俗文化財） * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]一宮の[[吉備津彦神社]]の[[吉備津彦神社の御田植祭|御田植祭]] *岡山県岡山市東区大多羅町の[[布勢神社|布施神社]]の御田植祭（岡山県指定重要無形民俗文化財）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=布施神社 お田植祭 {{!}} 岡山県北の生活情報 アットタウンWEBマガジン|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|website=|accessdate=2019-03-28|language=|publisher=}}</ref> * [[大分県]][[杵築市]]の[[若宮八幡社 (杵築市)|若宮八幡社]]の田植祭（[[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[伝統芸能]] * [[田楽]] * [[田植踊]] * [[御田舞]] * [[日本の祭一覧]]
== 参考文献 == * 『年中行事大辞典』（吉川弘文館、2009年）ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4 * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』（富山県[[教育委員会]] 生涯学習・文化財室）[[2007年]]（平成19年）3月発行
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf#search='%E5%AF%8C%E5%B1%B1%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%AD%E3%82%8A' 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』（富山県教育委員会 生涯学習・文化財室）]

[[ファイル:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|[[香取神宮御田植祭]]]] '''御田植祭'''（おたうえまつり）は、[[寺社]]や[[皇室]]の[[御田|領田]]で行われる行事である。
== 概要 == [[農作業]]の工程を模擬的に演じ[[豊作]]を祈念する[[神事]]であり、実際に[[田植え]]の時期に行われるものを御田植祭と呼び、[[正月|新春]]の[[予祝芸能]]を[[田遊び]]と呼ぶのが一般的であるが、田遊びを含め御田植祭と呼ばれることも多い。
この種の行事は全国に[[伝承]]されており、[[日本人]]と[[稲作]]との長く深い係わりを示すものとされる。地域によって行事を行う組織は異なるが、収穫までの全生産過程を演じる例は少なく、田植で終わる「御田植祭」が多いといわれる。
== 起源 == 苦しい田植えの作業を楽しくする方法として[[田植歌]]を歌いながら田植えをするという[[風習]]が、[[田の神]]を祀って豊穣を願う農耕儀礼と結びついて[[祭礼]]となったのが起源とされるが、歴史的には[[権門]]による勧農があり、その模倣や展開の結果として多くの地域で行われるようになったともいわれる。このため、地域を代表する古くからの寺社の[[年中行事]]として伝えられる場合が多く、いずれも農作業の工程を模擬的に演ずることが中心だが、地域ごとに種々の芸能の要素が伝えられ、特徴ある行事が伝承されている。
== 代表的な御田植祭 == === 日本三大御田植 === * [[千葉県]][[香取市]]香取の[[香取神宮]]の[[香取神宮御田植祭]]<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html 香取神宮の御田植祭] 香取市</ref> * [[三重県]][[志摩市]]の[[伊雑宮]]の御田植（[[磯部の御神田]]）（[[重要無形民俗文化財]]）<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 伊雑宮 御田植祭／日本三大御田植祭（重要無形民俗文化財）] 志摩市観光協会</ref> * [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]住吉の[[住吉大社]]の[[住吉の御田植]]（重要無形民俗文化財）<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== その他の御田植 === * [[福島県]][[東白川郡]][[棚倉町]]八槻の[[都々古別神社の御田植]]（重要無形民俗文化財） * 福島県[[喜多方市]]の[[慶徳稲荷神社]]の御田植祭（重要無形民俗文化財） * 福島県[[大沼郡]][[会津美里町]]の[[伊佐須美神社]]の御田植祭（重要無形民俗文化財） * [[埼玉県]][[秩父市]]番場町の[[秩父神社]]の[[秩父神社御田植祭]]（[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|埼玉県指定無形民俗文化財]]） * [[富山県]][[射水市]]の[[加茂神社 (射水市)|下村加茂神社]]の御田植祭（[[富山県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|富山県指定無形民俗文化財]]、[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）に「とやまの文化財百選（とやまの祭り百選部門）」に選定） * [[兵庫県]][[姫路市]][[広嶺山]]の[[広峯神社]]の御田植祭（姫路市指定無形民俗文化財） * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]一宮の[[吉備津彦神社]]の[[吉備津彦神社の御田植祭|御田植祭]] *岡山県岡山市東区大多羅町の[[布勢神社|布施神社]]の御田植祭（岡山県指定重要無形民俗文化財）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=布施神社 お田植祭 {{!}} 岡山県北の生活情報 アットタウンWEBマガジン|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|website=|accessdate=2019-03-28|language=|publisher=}}</ref> * [[大分県]][[杵築市]]の[[若宮八幡社 (杵築市)|若宮八幡社]]の田植祭（[[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]]）

{{DEFAULTSORT:おたうえまつり}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:農業に関するイベント]] [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:仏教行事]]

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Taue Odori|ja|田植踊|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Rice Cultivation]] to [[Category:Rice production]] moving to category that actually exists on enwiki using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Taue Odori|ja|田植踊|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

[[ja:御田植祭]] [[sn:Otaue Festival]] [[sn:taue Festival]]
<!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival-->

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|qq=}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|qq=}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bumper crop|qq=}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|qq=}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|qq=}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ta no kami|qq=}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|qq=}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsukowake Shrine|qq=}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} (Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|qq=}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ta no kami|qq=}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|qq=}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsukowake Shrine|qq=}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakata|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isasumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine|qq=}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkita|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dengaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of festivals in Japan|qq=}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|qq=}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|qq=}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bumper crop|qq=}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|qq=}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|qq=}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ta no kami|qq=}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|qq=}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsukowake Shrine|qq=}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakata|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isasumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine|qq=}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkita|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dengaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of festivals in Japan|qq=}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|qq=}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|qq=}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bumper crop|qq=}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|qq=}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|qq=}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ta no kami|qq=}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|qq=}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Izawa-no-miya|lt=Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsukowake Shrine|qq=}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakata|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isasumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine|qq=}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkita|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dengaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of festivals in Japan|qq=}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).

[[ja:御田植祭]] [[sn:Otaue Festival]] [[sn:taue Festival]]
<!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival-->

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|qq=}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|qq=}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bumper crop|qq=}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|qq=}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|qq=}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ta no kami|qq=}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|qq=}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Izawa-no-miya|lt=Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsukowake Shrine|qq=}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitakata|ja|喜多方市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakata|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isasumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine|qq=}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|lt=Kibitsuhiko Shrine|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkita|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dengaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of festivals in Japan|qq=}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

[[ja:御田植祭]] [[sn:Otaue Festival]] [[sn:taue Festival]]
<!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival--><!--Otaue Festival-->

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|qq=}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|qq=}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|lt=bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bumper crop|qq=}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|lt=New Year|ja|正月|1=Japanese New Year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Year|qq=}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|qq=}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|ta no kami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ta no kami|qq=}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|qq=}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Jingū Otaue Festival|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Izawa-no-miya|lt=Izawa-no-Miya|ja|伊雑宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shima|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, also known as the {{ill|Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|ja|磯部の御神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|lt=Sumiyoshi-taisha|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ward|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|ja|都々古別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsukowake Shrine|qq=}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanakura|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|qq=}} in Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isasumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|lt=Kibitsuhiko Shrine|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkita|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."

{{ill|Chichibu|ja|秩父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Imizu|ja|射水市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峯神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Himeji|ja|姫路市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|lt=Kibitsuhiko Shrine|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōkita|ja|東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkita|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dengaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of festivals in Japan|qq=}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]
