Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanakura}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kitakata, Fukushima|en|Kitakata, Fukushima|WD=Q496506|lt=Kitakata}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|WD=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加茂神社 (射水市)}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|WD=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|WD=Q209801|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|WD=Q500763|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Ōkita}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡神社|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja|comment=changed title based on Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|WD=Q3022455|lt=Dengaku}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|en|List of festivals in Japan|WD=Q10312376|lt=List of festivals in Japan}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御田植祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御田植祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御田植祭]]
{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農業|1=agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanakura}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kitakata, Fukushima|en|Kitakata, Fukushima|WD=Q496506|lt=Kitakata}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|WD=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加茂神社 (射水市)}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|WD=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|WD=Q209801|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|WD=Q500763|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Ōkita}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡神社|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616258}} in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|WD=Q3022455|lt=Dengaku}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|en|List of festivals in Japan|WD=Q10312376|lt=List of festivals in Japan}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanakura}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kitakata, Fukushima|en|Kitakata, Fukushima|WD=Q496506|lt=Kitakata}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|WD=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加茂神社 (射水市)}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|WD=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|WD=Q209801|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|WD=Q500763|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Ōkita}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡神社|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616258}} in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|WD=Q3022455|lt=Dengaku}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|en|List of festivals in Japan|WD=Q10312376|lt=List of festivals in Japan}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農業|1=agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, == Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農業|1=agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanakura}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kitakata, Fukushima|en|Kitakata, Fukushima|WD=Q496506|lt=Kitakata}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|WD=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加茂神社 (射水市)}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|WD=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|WD=Q209801|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|WD=Q500763|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Ōkita}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡神社|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616258}} in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|WD=Q3022455|lt=Dengaku}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|en|List of festivals in Japan|WD=Q10312376|lt=List of festivals in Japan}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/maturi/maturi.pdf Toyama Cultural Heritage 100 (Festivals Section) - Toyama Prefecture Board of Education]

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農業|1=Agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest|1=Bumper crop}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year|1=Japanese New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami|1=Ta-no-Kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=Annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya|1=Izawa-no-miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine|1=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加茂神社 (射水市)}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|WD=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine|1=Hiromine Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|WD=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|WD=Q500763|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|1=Kibitsuhiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Ōkita|1=東區}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡神社|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja|comment=changed title based on (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農業|1=Agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest|1=Bumper crop}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year|1=Japanese New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami|1=Ta-no-Kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=Annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya|1=Izawa-no-miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|{{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}}]] '''Otaue Matsuri''' (御田植祭) refers to ceremonies conducted at {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} or on {{ill|imperial estates|ja|領田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial estates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to celebrate rice planting.
== Overview == This is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} that involves reenacting the steps of {{ill|Agriculture|lt=agricultural work|ja|農作業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農業|1=Agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451}} to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest|1=Bumper crop}}. Ceremonies held during the actual rice planting season are called "Otaue Matsuri," while those held during {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=New Year|1=Japanese New Year}} as celebratory pre-harvest performances are generally known as "Tan-asobi." However, the term "Otaue Matsuri" often encompasses both.
These events are widespread across Japan, reflecting the Japanese people's long and deep connection with rice cultivation. While the organizations conducting these ceremonies vary by region, few rituals include all stages of agricultural production. Most focus solely on rice planting and are termed "Otaue Matsuri."
== Origins == The origin of Otaue Matsuri is believed to lie in the custom of singing {{ill|rice-planting songs|ja|田植歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice-planting songs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to make the physically demanding work of planting rice more enjoyable. Over time, this practice merged with agrarian rituals to pray to {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Ta no kami|1=Ta-no-Kami}} (field deities) for fertility, developing into a formal festival. Historically, the encouragement of agriculture by powerful landowners led to the spread and adaptation of these ceremonies across various regions. Consequently, these rituals are often transmitted as {{ill|annual events|ja|年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual events|1=Annual event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483816}} at prominent regional temples and shrines. While reenacting agricultural work remains central, unique regional cultural elements, including traditional performing arts, have been incorporated.
== Prominent Otaue Matsuri == === Japan’s Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals === * {{ill|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|ja|香取神宮御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="katori">[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/sightseeing/gyoji/haru/s_otaue.html Katori Jingū Otaue Matsuri] - Katori City</ref> * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Izawa-no-Miya|1=Izawa-no-miya}} in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, also known as the {{ill| Isobe-no-Omita|ja|磯部の御神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe-no-Ogōn-den|qq=}} (designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="shima">[https://www.kanko-shima.com/html/event_det.php?no=20150505021458 Izawa-no-Miya Otaue Festival: One of Japan's Three Great Rice-Planting Festivals] - Shima Tourism Association</ref> * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}’s {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|ja|住吉の御田植|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Otaue Festival|qq=}}, in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}} (also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property).<ref name="katori" /><ref name="shima" />
=== Other Notable Otaue Festivals === * {{ill|Tsutsukowake Shrine|en|Tsutsukowake Shrine|WD=Q11643869|lt=Tsutsukowake Shrine|1=Tsutsukowake Shrine}}’s Otaue Festival in {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanakura|1=Tanagura, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * The Otaue Festival of {{ill|Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja|慶徳稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitoku Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kitakata, Fukushima|en|Kitakata, Fukushima|WD=Q496506|lt=Kitakata|1=Kitakata, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine|1=Isasumi Shrine}} in {{ill|Aizu Misato|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Misato|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Fukushima Prefecture (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property). * {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|WD=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine|1=Chichibu Shrine}}’s {{ill|Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|ja|秩父神社御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Shrine Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} in {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|WD=Q647079|lt=Chichibu|1=Chichibu, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} (designated as a Saitama Prefectural Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja|下村加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-mura Kamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 加茂神社 (射水市)}} in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu|1=Imizu}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}} (designated as a Toyama Prefectural Cultural Property and selected for the Toyama Cultural Heritage 100). * {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|WD=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine|1=Hiromine Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|WD=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (designated as a Himeji City Intangible Cultural Property). * {{ill|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|en|Kibitsuhiko Shrine|WD=Q500763|lt=Kibitsu-hiko Shrine|1=Kibitsuhiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Ōkita|1=東區}}, Okayama Prefecture (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://afw-at.com/page_e.php?id=432|title=Fuse Shrine Otaue Festival|publisher=At Town Web Magazine|accessdate=2019-03-28}}</ref> * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja|若宮八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kitsuki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若宮八幡神社|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616258}} in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki|1=Kitsuki, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} (designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property).
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=Theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|WD=Q3022455|lt=Dengaku|1=Dengaku}} * {{ill|Rice-planting dance|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taue Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Ota-mai|ja|御田舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota-mai|qq=}} * {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|en|List of festivals in Japan|WD=Q10312376|lt=List of festivals in Japan|1=List of festivals in Japan}}
== Further Reading == * 『年中行事大辞典』 (Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Annual Events) (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2009). ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4. * 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(3) とやまの祭り』 (Toyama’s 100 Cultural Treasures, Series 3: Festivals) (Toyama Prefectural Board of Education, 2007).
